learning_outcomes.description: Synthesize your experience of industry practices, including ethics and relevant codes of conduct through oral, written and other relevant media.
Understand and effectively perform various assigned professional communication activities, including research, writing, coordinating; reflect on the application of theory to practice.

Analyse and critically reflect on how your knowledge of communications relates to the professional context of the workplace and then communicate these to a professional standard through oral, written, and other media.
Compile the written and digital tools necessary to gain employment

assessments.task_description:
You are required to complete a minimum of 50 work hours in a School-approved placement, undertaking those tasks assigned to you and seeking to display initiative, enthusiasm, professionalism and a willingness to learn. You will, where opportunity allows, contribute to the output of the organisation, working under the supervision of a communication practitioner.

You can undertake your placement at any time during the Semester in which you are enrolled in COMU3801 and can attend on consecutive days (e.g. Monday - Friday), or several days a week spread over several weeks. Although not required, you can also undertake School approved placements at more than one organisation and/or for maximum of 30 work days for the Semester in which you are enrolled in COMU3801. Please let the Placements Officer know if you wish to alter the duration of your placement once it has commenced and they will update your insurance accordingly.

During your placement you are representing the University and are expected to abide by the Student Code of Conduct https://ppl.app.uq.edu.au/content/1.50.01-code-conduct

Your placement organisation has also entered into a Student Placement Agreement with UQ. This Agreement details the rights and obligations of you the Student, the University and the placement organisation (Provider) during your placement. Although instances are rare, if you have concerns regarding your treatment during your placement, you may wish to bring these up with your placement supervisor with reference to this Agreement; or discuss them with the Placements Officer (scaplacements@uq.edu.au or 07 3346 8286) for further advice. If you have any immediate concerns for your safety or well being during a placement, you can leave the work place immediately. Please let the Placements Officer know as soon as practical if this occurs.

Your passing mark for your Work Placement is determined by your placement supervisor signing off on your Work Plan (see Employment Portfolio assessment task) at the start of semester and when you have advised the placements coordinator by email (cc-ing in your supervisor) that you have completed a minimum of 50 placement hours.  You are required to send this email within 48 hours of having completed the minimum of 50 placement hours.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Please notify the Placement Officer when you have completed your required placement hours.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

assessments.assessment_title: Employment Portfolio - LinkedIn Profile and Work Plan
assessments.due_date:
27/07/2026 - 23/10/2026

LinkedIn Profile:

  • Recommended due date: 4pm 28 August 2026
  • Latest date to submit this assessment: 4pm 23 October 2026

Work Plan:

  • Recommended due date: Before your placement begins
  • Latest date to submit this assessment: 4pm 23 October 2026
assessments.category: Paper/ Report/ Annotation, Practical/ Demonstration, Portfolio
assessments.task_description:
LinkedIn Profile:

Students will receive training at the first workshop so that they can create a professional LinkedIn profile so that future employers can identify relevant professional experience, education, knowledge, and skills. This profile will become a crucial component of a student’s employability; it is what employers look at first when they are looking at an applicant. Following the first workshop, students will then create their own profile following the guidelines provided in the workshop and training. 

Work Plan & Code of Conduct:

The second part of assessment 1 involves signing a student code of conduct and developing a work plan for your placement. The two-page plan describes what you will do during your time at the placement. You are to set real and achievable goals in this plan. Please work with your placement supervisor to identify your goals and the steps needed to achieve them.

Requirements

Sign the student code of conduct and write a two-page work plan. This should include a description of what you will do, what you hope to achieve (goals, at least three). This work plan needs to be signed off by you and your placement supervisor.

assessments.assessment_title: Job Application - CV and Selection Criteria
assessments.due_date:
25/09/2026 - 23/10/2026

  • Recommended due date: 4pm 25 September 2026
  • Latest date to submit this assessment: 4pm 23 October 2026
assessments.task_description: During the second workshop, students will sharpen their CV writing skills as well as learn the best strategies for responding to the selection criteria on job applications. Students will then work on their CVs and write a cover letter. The course coordinator will provide three job options for selection criteria to which students must respond. Students are only required to answer one.

assessments.task_description: In the first few weeks of the semester, students will be matched with a journalism, communications or digital media mentor. During the semester students are to contact their mentor and interview them using the questions provided as a basis for reflecting their chosen profession. In the workshop in Week 12 students will meet with their industry mentors to gain industry insights. All students are expected to attend this important industry networking event.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom O-week To Exam week 2(20 Jul - 22 Nov)
learning_activities.topic:
Internship: Industry Placement

You are required to work for a minimum of 50 hours in an organisational placement. You must find your own placement and advice regarding this process is available from the Placements Officer. All of the placement must be undertaken during the semester in which you are enrolled on COMU3801; retrospective credit is not permitted. The performance and assessment requirements of COMU3801 cannot be used to vary the requirements of other courses. Please note that all placements MUST BE APPROVED by the course coordinator before commencement. *All students must arrange their placements around the 5 course workshops.


Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L04
learning_activities.topic_description: Internship: Industry Placement You are required to work for a minimum of 50 hours in an organisational placement. You must find your own placement and advice regarding this process is available from the Placements Officer. All of the placement must be undertaken during the semester in which you are enrolled on COMU3801; retrospective credit is not permitted. The performance and assessment requirements of COMU3801 cannot be used to vary the requirements of other courses. Please note that all placements MUST BE APPROVED by the course coordinator before commencement. *All students must arrange their placements around the 5 course workshops.

learning_activities.topic:
Workshop One: Course Overview

Introduction to the course, assessments and consider the ethics and practicalities use of AI in the workplace. *All students must arrange their placements around the 5 course workshops.


Learning outcomes: L02, L03, L04
learning_activities.topic_description: Workshop One: Course Overview Introduction to the course, assessments and consider the ethics and practicalities use of AI in the workplace. *All students must arrange their placements around the 5 course workshops.

learning_activities.topic:
Workshop Two: Professional Identity/Self Branding

In this workshop, students get advice on how to create a self brand and how to carry that logic over into their digital representation online in spaces such as LinkedIn. Students work directly in class with tutor support on the first assessment task. *All students must arrange their placements around the 5 course workshops.


Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03
learning_activities.topic_description: Workshop Two: Professional Identity/Self Branding In this workshop, students get advice on how to create a self brand and how to carry that logic over into their digital representation online in spaces such as LinkedIn. Students work directly in class with tutor support on the first assessment task. *All students must arrange their placements around the 5 course workshops.

learning_activities.topic:
Workshop Three: Resumes, CVs and Cover Letters

In this workshop, students apply for a job. Students learn how to draft a cover letter, create a CV and respond to selection criteria in preparation for the second assessment task. With tutor support students consider where AI use is and is not helpful. *All students must arrange their placements around the 5 course workshops.


Learning outcomes: L03
learning_activities.topic_title: Workshop Three: Resumes, CVs and Cover Letters
learning_activities.topic_description: Workshop Three: Resumes, CVs and Cover Letters In this workshop, students apply for a job. Students learn how to draft a cover letter, create a CV and respond to selection criteria in preparation for the second assessment task. With tutor support students consider where AI use is and is not helpful. *All students must arrange their placements around the 5 course workshops.

learning_activities.topic:
Workshop Four: Professional Reflection & Job Interviews

In this workshop students learn about the importance of reflective practice in preparation for the third assessment task. Students learn about the connection between reflective and job interviews. *All students must arrange their placements around the 5 course workshops.

Learning outcomes: L02
learning_activities.topic_description: Workshop Four: Professional Reflection & Job Interviews In this workshop students learn about the importance of reflective practice in preparation for the third assessment task. Students learn about the connection between reflective and job interviews. *All students must arrange their placements around the 5 course workshops.

learning_activities.topic:
Workshop Five: Professional Networking

In this workshop, students will take part in a networking event where they will meet with journalism, communications and digital media mentors from across the state. All students are expected to attend this important industry mentoring event. *All students must arrange their placements around the 5 course workshops.

Learning outcomes: L01, L03
